title: Conditionals review
explanation:
Conditionals review

Use the zero conditional for facts, the first conditional for real future possibilities, the second conditional for imaginary present or future situations, and the third conditional for unreal past situations.

questions.text: If you heat water to 100C, it [*boils|will boil|would boil].
If she studies hard, she [*will pass|would pass|passes] the exam.
If I were taller, I [*would play|will play|played] basketball.
If they had left earlier, they [*would have caught|would catch|caught] the train.
If you [*press|will press|pressed] this button, the machine starts.

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: If I ____ more time, I would travel more. | *had | have | will have
If he had listened, he ____ that mistake. | *would not have made | will not make | did not make
If the weather is nice, we ____ to the beach. | *will go | would go | go
If babies are hungry, they ____ . | *cry | will cry | would cry
If I [*had known|knew|would know] about the meeting, I would have come.
We [*will stay|would stay|stayed] inside if the storm gets worse.
If she [*were|was|is] more confident, she would speak in public.
If you mix yellow and blue, you [*get|will get|would get] green.
They would be happier if they [*lived|live|had lived] closer to their family.
